itw_related.shortDescription: A type confusion issue affecting multiple Apple products allows processing of maliciously crafted web content, leading to arbitrary code execution.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple Multiple Products Type Confusion Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: A type confusion issue affecting multiple Apple products allows processing of maliciously crafted web content, leading to arbitrary code execution.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple macOS Out-of-Bounds Write Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: macOS Monterey contains an out-of-bounds write vulnerability that could allow an application to execute arbitrary code with kernel privileges.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple macOS Out-of-Bounds Read Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: macOS Monterey contains an out-of-bounds read vulnerability that could allow an application to read kernel memory.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple iOS, iPadOS, and macOS Webkit Use-After-Free Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Apple iOS, iPadOS, and macOS WebKit contain a use-after-free vulnerability that leads to code execution when processing maliciously crafted web content. This vulnerability could impact HTML parsers that use WebKit, including but not limited to Apple Safari and non-Apple products which rely on WebKit for HTML processing.

patchmap.subject: Expand RefPtr / Ref use in FrameLoader

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple OS X Authentication Bypass Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: The XPC implementation in Admin Framework in Apple OS X before 10.10.3 allows local users to bypass authentication and obtain admin privileges.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple OS X Heap-Based Buffer Overflow Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Heap-based buffer overflow in IOHIDFamily in Apple OS X, which affects, iOS before 8 and Apple TV before 7, allows attackers to execute arbitrary code in a privileged context.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple Memory Corruption Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Apple IOMobileFrameBuffer contains a memory corruption vulnerability which can allow a malicious application to execute arbitrary code with kernel privileges.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple iOS, iPadOS, macOS Use-After-Free Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Apple iOS, iPadOS, and macOS WebKit contain a use-after-free vulnerability that leads to code execution when processing maliciously crafted web content. This vulnerability could impact HTML parsers that use WebKit, including but not limited to Apple Safari and non-Apple products which rely on WebKit for HTML processing.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple iOS and macOS Group Facetime Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Apple iOS and macOS Group FaceTime contains an unspecified vulnerability where the call initiator can cause the recipient's Apple device to answer unknowingly or without user interaction.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple Multiple Products Integer Overflow Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Apple iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and watchOS CoreGraphics contain an integer overflow vulnerability which may allow code execution when processing a maliciously crafted PDF. The vulnerability is also known under the moniker of FORCEDENTRY.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple Multiple Products Memory Corruption Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Apple iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and watchOS FontParser contain a memory corruption vulnerability which may allow for code execution when processing maliciously crafted front.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple Multiple Products Memory Corruption Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Apple iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and watchOS IOMobileFrameBuffer contain a memory corruption vulnerability which may allow an application to execute code with kernel privileges.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple Multiple Products Memory Initialization Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Apple iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and watchOS contain a memory initialization vulnerability that may allow a malicious application to disclose kernel memory.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple Multiple Products Type Confusion Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Apple iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and watchOS contain a type confusion vulnerability that may allow a malicious application to execute code with kernel privileges.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple iOS, iPadOS, and watchOS Out-of-Bounds Write Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Apple iOS, iPadOS, and watchOS Mail contains an out-of-bounds write vulnerability which may allow memory modification or application termination when processing a maliciously crafted mail message.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple iOS, iPadOS, and watchOS Memory Corruption Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Apple iOS, iPadOS, and watchOS Mail contains a memory corruption vulnerability that may allow heap corruption when processing a maliciously crafted mail message.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple iOS WebKit Use-After-Free Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Apple iOS WebKit contains a use-after-free vulnerability that leads to code execution when processing maliciously crafted web content. This vulnerability could impact HTML parsers that use WebKit, including but not limited to Apple Safari and non-Apple products which rely on WebKit for HTML processing.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple Multiple Products Race Condition Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Apple iOS, iPadOs, macOS, watchOS, and tvOS contain a race condition vulnerability that may allow a malicious application to elevate privileges.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Apple iOS, iPadOS, and macOS WebKit Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Apple iOS, iPadOS, and macOS WebKit contain an unspecified logic vulnerability that allows a remote attacker to execute code. This vulnerability could impact HTML parsers that use WebKit, including but not limited to Apple Safari and non-Apple products which rely on WebKit for HTML processing.

This vulnerability could impact HTML parsers that use WebKit, including but not limited to Apple Safari and non-Apple products which rely on WebKit for HTML processing.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Mozilla Multiple Products Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Mozilla Firefox, SeaMonkey, and Thunderbird contain an unspecified vulnerability when JavaScript is enabled. This allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via vectors related to nsCSSFrameConstructor::ContentAppended, the appendChild method, incorrect index tracking, and the creation of multiple frames, which triggers memory corruption.

patchmap.subject: Bug 607222.  Start our update batches a little earlier.  Patches by smaug and sicking, r=smaug on sicking's code, r=sicking on smaug's code, r=me on the whole thing, a=clegnitto

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Mozilla Firefox Use-After-Free Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Mozilla Firefox and Firefox ESR contain a use-after-free vulnerability in Animation timelines that allows for code execution in the content process.

patchmap.subject: Bug 1923344 - r=smaug

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Mozilla Firefox, Firefox ESR, and Thunderbird Use-After-Free Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Mozilla Firefox, Firefox ESR, and Thunderbird contain a use-after-free vulnerability in SVG Animation, targeting Firefox and Tor browser users on Windows.

patchmap.subject: Bug 1321066 - Explicitly guard against reentrance in nsSMILTimeContainer. r=dholbert

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Mozilla Firefox Security Feature Bypass Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Moxilla Firefox allows remote attackers to bypass the Same Origin Policy to read arbitrary files or gain privileges.

patchmap.subject: Bug 1179262 - Remove PlayPreview usage from PDF viewer. r=bz

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Mozilla Firefox and Thunderbird Type Confusion Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Mozilla Firefox and Thunderbird contain a type confusion vulnerability that can occur when manipulating JavaScript objects due to issues in Array.pop, allowing for an exploitable crash.

patchmap.subject: Bug 1544386 part 1 - Call ElementAccessHasExtraIndexedProperty instead of ArrayPrototypeHasIndexedProperty when inlining array natives. r=tcampbell

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Mozilla Firefox and Thunderbird Sandbox Escape Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Mozilla Firefox and Thunderbird contain a sandbox escape vulnerability that could result in remote code execution.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Mozilla Firefox and Thunderbird Denial-of-Service Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Mozilla Firefox and Thunderbird do not properly handle onreadystatechange events in conjunction with page reloading, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial-of-service (DoS) or possibly execute malicious code via a crafted web site.

patchmap.subject: Bug 857883, don't assert about readyState when not needed, r=bz

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Mozilla Firefox Use-After-Free Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Mozilla Firefox contains a use-after-free vulnerability in WebGPU IPC Framework which can be exploited to perform arbitrary code execution.

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Mozilla Firefox Use-After-Free Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Mozilla Firefox contains a use-after-free vulnerability in XSLT parameter processing which can be exploited to perform arbitrary code execution.

patchmap.subject: Bug 1758062 - Convert parameters upfront. r=smaug, a=tritter

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Mozilla Firefox Information Disclosure Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Mozilla Firefox does not properly initialize data structures for the nsDOMSVGZoomEvent::mPreviousScale and nsDOMSVGZoomEvent::mNewScale functions, which allows remote attackers to obtain sensitive information from process memory via a crafted web site.

patchmap.subject: Bug 866825 - Initialize nsDOMSVGZoomEvent::m{Previous,New}Scale; r=smaug

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Mozilla Firefox And Thunderbird Use-After-Free Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Mozilla Firefox and Thunderbird contain a race condition vulnerability when running the nsDocShell destructor under certain conditions. The race condition creates a use-after-free vulnerability, causing unspecified impacts.

patchmap.subject: Bug 1620818 - Release nsDocShell::mContentViewer properly. r=nika,peterv a=dveditz

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Mozilla Firefox And Thunderbird Use-After-Free Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Mozilla Firefox and Thunderbird contain a race condition vulnerability when handling a ReadableStream under certain conditions. The race condition creates a use-after-free vulnerability, causing unspecified impacts.

patchmap.subject: Bug 1626728 - Normalize shutdown. r=perry,jstutte, a=RyanVM

itw_related.vulnerabilityName: Mozilla Firefox And Thunderbird Type Confusion Vulnerability
itw_related.shortDescription: Mozilla Firefox and Thunderbird contain a type confusion vulnerability due to incorrect alias information in the IonMonkey JIT compiler when setting array elements.
